title: Plan
description: Orchestration plan metadata. Narrative lives in plan.md frontmatter; this schema validates that frontmatter.

quota_caps.description: Per-model % weekly quota cap for this plan. OSS is unmetered (Ollama local).

hard_stop.description: true = terminate at cap; false (default) = pause for human approval via INBOX.md, reset counters on approval.

goal_type.description: Goal-completion governance category. When set to one of {parity, new_feature, migration, incident}, the plan must declare links.objective_contract pointing at an ObjectiveContract file. mechanical/infra/refactor plans don't require a contract; backward-compatible default is null/absent.

surfaces.description: v1.7.0 — added external-api-wrap surface for plans whose goal is wrapping an existing OpenAPI-shaped service; consume via the printing-press-adapter skill (DontPanic claude/skills/).

protected_paths.description: Path patterns that require human approval to modify

items.description: Plan IDs this plan depends on

objective_contract.description: Path reference (relative to plan dir) to an ObjectiveContract JSON file. Required when goal_type ∈ {parity, new_feature, migration, incident}; the plan-level validator enforces presence + file-exists + ObjectiveContract validation.

orchestration.description: v1.9.0 — child-plan nested-orchestration metadata. Absent on top-level plans; populated on child plans spawned by a parent. Example (from v4.1 plan 2026-05-12-002): {parent_plan_id: '2026-05-11-001-infra-state-projection-adapters-meta', spawn_reason: 'operator_manual', depth_limit: 3}.

parent_plan_id.description: Plan ID of the parent plan that spawned this child. Example: '2026-05-11-001-infra-state-projection-adapters-meta'.

spawn_reason.description: Why this child plan exists. 'operator_manual' = operator-authored child plan (the v4.1 + v4 + dashboard-adapter pattern). 'auto' = automated spawn (e.g., supervisor / triage classifier). 'test' = synthetic plan used by fixtures + integration tests.

depth_limit.description: Per-plan depth cap. Frontmatter may declare a LOWER value than the platform default; higher values are rejected by the runtime. Example: 3 (the v4.1 default).

child_charter.description: v1.9.0 — child-plan charter declaring bounded work, parent objective, allowed paths, and return condition. Only valid when orchestration.parent_plan_id is set. Example (from v4.1 plan 2026-05-12-002): {kind: 'implementation', parent_objective: 'Discharge v4.1 carry items…', parent_acceptance_item: 'v4 D008 + D009…', allowed_paths: ['scripts/dontpanic_orchestrate/**', 'claude/shared/**'], forbidden_decisions: ['Do not break the audit.schema.json contract…'], return_condition_summary: 'F001 + F002 pass; sweep ≥1928 green…', may_edit_product_code: true}.

kind.description: Child plan classification. 'implementation' = ship product/runtime code (the v4.1 default). 'investigation' = scoped probe that produces evidence/notes but does not ship code. 'migration' = bounded schema/data/dependency migration.

parent_objective.description: What the parent needed when it spawned this child (≤500 chars). Example: 'Discharge v4.1 carry items banked during the v4 plan close-out…'.

parent_acceptance_item.description: The parent acceptance clause this child unblocks. Example: 'v4 D008 (parsing-category schema mismatch), v4 D009…'.

allowed_paths.description: fnmatch-style glob patterns. When commit_policy.mode='child_commit', the runtime requires every modified file to match at least one glob. Example: ['scripts/dontpanic_orchestrate/**', 'claude/shared/**'].

forbidden_decisions.description: Free-form decisions the child must not take (informational; not machine-enforced). Example: ['Do not break the audit.schema.json contract for existing fields…'].

return_condition_summary.description: One short text describing the observable signal used to render the `## Return Condition` section template in the close-out memo. Example: 'F001 + F002 pass; sweep ≥1928 green…'.

may_edit_product_code.description: Whether the child is allowed to modify product code. NO default — operator must declare intent explicitly. Cross-field invariant with commit_policy.mode enforced by the runtime. Example: true.

commit_policy.description: v1.9.0 — commit authority for a child plan. Example (from v4.1 plan 2026-05-12-002): {mode: 'child_commit', requires: ['tests_pass', 'evidence_packaged']}.

mode.description: 'child_commit' = child writes its own commits within allowed_paths (the v4.1 default). 'parent_commit' = child produces evidence and the parent plan lands the commit. 'manual' = operator commits by hand outside the supervisor.

requires.description: Compliance checks evaluated at close-out. Plain strings so the runtime can extend the vocabulary without a schema bump. Example: ['tests_pass', 'evidence_packaged'].

external_refs.description: v1.10.0 — opt-in pointers to external entities the plan tracks (PM-tool issues, etc.). DontPanic plan lifecycle hooks (lock validates reachability, close pushes status) consult these via the category-adapter layer (scripts/dontpanic_orchestrate/integrations/). Strictly additive — absent or empty array = no external sync. Example: [{kind: 'pm_issue', uri: 'linear://issue/ABC-123', sync: 'push_status'}].

kind.description: Category tag. v0 ships 'pm_issue' only (PM-tool category contract from plan 2026-05-20-001 F001). New kinds require expanding the category contract.

uri.description: Canonical reference URI: <scheme>://<entity_type>/<id>. Scheme matches a category-adapter wrapper's uri_scheme (e.g. 'linear://issue/ABC-123', 'linear://project/TEAM-7'). The pattern is permissive on the id segment — vendor-native identifiers vary widely.

sync.description: Outbound sync opt-in. 'none' (default at semantic level) = ref is informational only; lock-time reachability failures DO NOT block lock. 'push_status' = plan close pushes DontPanic status flip to the external entity; lock-time reachability failures block lock loud.
